kill_fact: No clean kill: every load-bearing figure verifies against the primary Q2 FY26 release (SEC 8-K / PR Newswire, May 6 2026) and the Bosch 8-K (Aug 1 2025). The bear case (already-fired catalyst + no margin of safety) is itself CONFIRMED, not refuted — but it argues 'fairly-valued, poor entry,' not a broken thesis. The one factual defect: the dossier/bull's 'Americas +310bp' is wrong — Americas adj segment EBITA margin rose only +100bp YoY (19.5%); the +310bp figure is the TOTAL adj segment margin to 15.5%, not Americas. EMEA +370bp / APAC +350bp verify. This does not break the case but the per-region margin-inflection claim is overstated for Americas (the 'crown jewel').
corrections: (1) Americas margin expansion is +100bp YoY (not +310bp); +310bp is the consolidated adj segment EBITA margin to 15.5%. (2) The '70% of backlog converts within 12 months' and the 'NVIDIA thermal reference-architecture collaboration' claims are NOT in the official earnings release — they trace to transcript/IR commentary, not the primary press release; treat as management-asserted, not press-release-verified. (3) Bosch net proceeds ~$5.0B after tax verify, but the headline $8.1B is the TOTAL deal; JCI's gross consideration was ~$6.7B — use the net (~$5.0B) for the ASR-funding math. (4) Current price ~$146 (Jun 2026), ATH $147.75 on Jun 9 2026 verifies; the SoP base ~$145 sits AT market — risk/reward ~1:1, catalyst already fired. Cap conviction accordingly.
evidence: SEC Form 8-K / PR Newswire, 'Johnson Controls Reports Strong Q2 Results; Raises FY26 Guidance,' May 6 2026 — Q2 sales $6.1B, organic +6%, orders +30% organic, backlog $20.0B (+26% organic; Americas $14.9B / EMEA $3.2B / APAC $1.9B), adj EPS $1.19 (+45%), adj segment EBITA margin 15.5%, Americas +100bp/EMEA +370bp/APAC +350bp, net debt 2.0x, ~100% FCF conversion, FY26 guide raised to ~$4.85 adj EPS / ~6% organic / ~50% leverage.

Johnson Controls press release / SEC 8-K, 'Completes Sale of Residential and Light Commercial HVAC Business,' Aug 1 2025 — $8.1B total deal value, JCI consideration ~$6.7B, ~$5.0B net cash after tax, $5.0B accelerated share repurchase program announced.

Macrotrends / stockanalysis.com price history (accessed Jun 2026) — JCI all-time-high close $147.75 on Jun 9 2026; price ~$146, mkt cap ~$89B; forward P/E ~25-27x, EV/EBITDA ~22-28x (source-dependent) — full multiple at all-time highs, confirming the 'priced-in / no margin of safety' bear.

Q2 FY26 release reconciliation — orders +30% organic vs FY26 organic revenue guide only ~6%: the orders-to-revenue conversion gap is real and management-acknowledged, confirming the catalyst is a deferred, customer-controlled (hyperscaler-capex-gated) option, not current revenue.
